title: Cisco ISE — MDM/UEM Connector Health and Compliance Drift

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 12.2 — endpoint security policies. MDM compliance check is one such control.
Art.21(2)(d) covers access control. MDM-driven access control is a primary mechanism.
AC-19 (Access Control for Mobile Devices) — direct alignment.
A.8.1 (User endpoint devices) — MDM is the user endpoint device control.
164.310(d)(1) Device and media controls — MDM compliance is one such control.
compliance.controlObjective: Mobile device controls for ePHI environments.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Revoke ISE's API token in the MDM admin console. ISE emits 60142 on next API call. UC-17.1.50 fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Restore the token. Connection re-establishes. Alert clears.

dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *MDM Server* category — codes `60140` (MDM compliance update), `60141` (MDM connection lost), `60142` (MDM auth failed), `60143` (MDM API rate-limit), `60144` (compliance status updated for endpoint). Vendors supported by ISE 3.x MDM API: Microsoft Intune, VMware Workspace ONE, Jamf, MobileIron, Citrix Endpoint Management, IBM MaaS360, BlackBerry UEM. Field: `mdm_vendor`, `mdm_compliance`, `mdm_registered`, `mdm_jailbroken`. ISE OpenAPI `/api/v1/mdm/server/status` polled every 10 min.
spl:
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (60140, 60141, 60142, 60143, 60144))
  OR (index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:mdm_status")
| stats count latest(_time) as last_event count(eval(MessageCode=="60141")) as drops count(eval(MessageCode=="60142")) as auth_failures count(eval(MessageCode=="60143")) as rate_limits by mdm_vendor
| where drops > 1 OR auth_failures > 0 OR rate_limits > 5
description: Detects MDM/UEM connector failures, auth issues, and rate-limit hits on ISE-to-MDM API integrations. Without MDM connector health, ISE authorization decisions that depend on `mdm_compliance` or `mdm_jailbroken` revert to non-compliance default (typically more permissive).
value: BYOD environments rely on MDM compliance to gate corporate-resource access. A broken Intune connector means non-compliant devices receive the same access as compliant ones. Detecting integration failures within 10 minutes preserves compliance enforcement.
implementation: Confirm *MDM Server* category at INFO. Deploy OpenAPI poller. Schedule every 10 min. Throttle suppression on `mdm_vendor` for 30 min.
visualization: (1) Per-vendor connector status. (2) Compliance distribution panel. (3) Top-N: rate-limit hits by vendor.
knownFalsePositives: (a) **MDM vendor maintenance** — vendor-side maintenance windows. (b) **Token refresh window** — Some MDM APIs require token refresh; brief auth_failures during the refresh.
mitreAttack: N/A (MDM integration health — measures ISE-to-UEM connection state, no attack mapping)
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• MDM connector configured.
• `mdm_vendors.csv` lookup with `vendor`, `is_critical`.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable *MDM Server* category at INFO. Deploy OpenAPI poller.

Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field. Schedule every 10 min.

Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as above.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — MDM Connector Health":
• Per-vendor status.
• Compliance distribution.
• Top-N rate-limit hits.

Alerting: PagerDuty medium on connector failure.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Auth failures recur** — Token rotation needed.
• **Rate limits sustained** — Tune ISE's MDM polling cadence.
• **`mdm_compliance` always 'unknown'** — MDM provider doesn't expose the field; check provider model.
grandmaExplanation: If a phone or laptop is enrolled in the company's mobile-management system and that system says 'this device is not OK', ISE should block it. We watch the conversation between ISE and the management system — and ring the bell when that conversation breaks.
